entries.definition: Credit pressure means lenders may stop helping unless leaders show stronger fiscal control.
entries.why_it_matters: Fiscal credibility shapes which policy choices remain possible.

entries.definition: Service cuts reduce public spending by shrinking what the city provides.
entries.why_it_matters: Cuts can save money, but they also change trust, labor stability, and neighborhood strain.

entries.definition: Bondholders are investors who expect the city to repay borrowed money.
entries.why_it_matters: Their support affects whether the city can buy time during fiscal crisis.

background_notes: This pack binds the canonical systems to fiscal-crisis language around budget pressure, labor bargaining, and visible service cuts.
